title: "Get events" API endpoint only returns a single result if streaming isn't used
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
Hello,
the [get events API endpoint](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/SystemEventsLibpod) only returns a single result if streaming isn't used. If this is intended, it doesn't seem to be documented.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start podman API.

2. Request events with curl: ``curl -X GET --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock "http://d/v3.1.2/libpod/events?since=60m&until=1622039711674168800&stream=false"`` 

3. Compare to output of streaming request: ``curl -X GET --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock "http://d/v3.1.2/libpod/events?since=60m&until=1622039711674168800"`` 

**Describe the results you received:**
Requests with `stream=0` or `stream=false` result in a single response while the streaming equivalent over the same duration returns a lot more results.


**Describe the results you expected:**
The same amount of results for requests with streaming and without.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Output of the requests:
```
alpaca@DESKTOP-3COMHL6:/mnt/c/Users/alpaca/Downloads$ curl -X GET --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock "http://d/v3.1.2/libpod/events?since=60m&until=1622039711674168800&stream=false"
{"Type":"image","Action":"pull","Actor":{"ID":"","Attributes":{"containerExitCode":"0","image":"","name":""}},"scope":"local","time":1622038780,"timeNano":1622038780931954200}
```


I'm not sure whether the stream should terminate after the `until` time is reached, it doesn't as far as I can tell.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Windows 10, 64bit, WSL2, Debian 10

comments.body:
> I'm not sure whether the stream should terminate after the until time is reached, it doesn't as far as I can tell.

After some more tests, it does seem to terminate at the appropriate time.

I found some other combinations that don't work as expected:
* `podman events --since=5h --until=1m` and `` curl -X GET --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock "http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/events?since=5h&until=1m"`` immediately return without output.  `podman events --since=5h` does return multiple entries.
* `podman events --until=1m` returns one entry: `2021-04-11 19:01:01.2912941 +0200 CEST image pull`, 
``curl -X GET --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock "http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/events?until=1m"`` returns the same entry `{"Type":"image","Action":"pull","Actor":{"ID":"","Attributes":{"containerExitCode":"0","image":"","name":""}},"scope":"local","time":1618160461,"timeNano":1618160461291294100}`
* At 18:52 UTC: `podman events --since=2006-01-02T15:04:05 --until=2021-05-26T18:50:55` returns `2021-04-11 19:01:01.2912941 +0200 CEST image pull` while `podman events --since=2006-01-02T15:04:05 --until=2021-05-26T18:54:55` returns the real logs with a huge amount of entries. Same with the API.

Not sure if this is related or a different issue.
This has been fixed in the main branch by commit 86610c7852ee992d6c0b89d4a5cd70081950951d.  Thanks for opening the issue!
